perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is to be construed without regard to the party or parties responsible for its drafting or preparation. The section headings and captions contained in this Agreement are for convenience only and do not affect the construction or interpretation of any provision of this Agreement. No amendment, waiver or modification of this Agreement is binding unless it is in a writing that explicitly references this Agreement and is signed by authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is to be construed without regard to the party or parties responsible for its drafting or preparation. The section headings and captions contained in this Agreement are for convenience only and do not affect the construction or interpretation of any provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "No amendment, waiver or modification of this Agreement is binding unless it is in a writing that explicitly references this Agreement and is signed by authorized representatives of both parties.", it creates an uncertainty about how the contract may be changed, thus creating an internal contradiction. This creates ambiguity regarding how modifications to the agreement can be legally made. Without this it contradicts the standard legal practice of requiring written and signed agreements for any changes to a contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is to be construed without regard to the party or parties responsible for its drafting or preparation. The section headings and captions contained in this Agreement are for convenience only and do not affect the construction or interpretation of any provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that any amendment, waiver, or modification required a written and signed agreement. The changed text omits this clause entirely. The contradicted text does not explicitly say how to modify the agreement but without the omitted clause, the agreement now lacks any guidance on how modifications can be made, creating an implicit contradiction because it goes against standard legal practices for contracts to require written and signed amendments.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Confidential Information to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Confidential Information required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. A disclosure of Confidential Information required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Omitting "Prior to disclosure of Confidential Information to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates." from Section 5 creates a contradiction, as there is no longer a clause to protect Discloser when the Recipient is sharing information with outside parties. This contradicts Section 2 which outlines what Confidential Information is as Recipient is not able to share with anyone without the appropriate protection, yet Section 5 doesn't provide this protection anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information disclosed by or on behalf of a party (“Discloser”) and/or its Affiliates during the term of this Agreement which is received, learned, or observed by the other party (“Recipient”) and/or its Affiliates is “Confidential Information,” including, but not limited to, business and financial information, marketing and strategic plans, customer lists, employee lists, reports, technologies, proc esses and operations, compilations, analyses, forecasts, studies, summaries, notes, data, product designs, drawings, prints, specifications, production schedules, test data, operations, inventions, know -how, component and part designs, schematics, formulat ions, samples, passwords, machines, network IDs, prototypes, mock -ups, templates, processing and control information, product performance data, manuals, purchase and sales records, warranty and repair records, marketing information, quotes and pricing info rmation, computer programs, or the like, including e- mail and electronic communication discussing any of the foregoing. Notwithstanding the forgoing, Confidential Information shall not include any information that is: (a) already lawfully in the possession of or known to Recipient as of the Effective Date without any obligation of confidentiality; (b) already in the public domain at the time of disclosure to Recipient or which, after such disclosure, enters into the public domain through no fault of Recipient; (c) lawfully furnished or disclosed to Recipient by a non- party to this Agreement without any obligation of confidentiality and through no wrongful act of Recipient; (d) independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Informati on; or (e) explicitly approved for public release by Discloser. GISF- 032 A - Non-Disclosure Agreement.doc Page 1 of 4 The foregoing list of exceptions is exhaustive and shall be strictly construed. In cases of doubt as to the use of Confidential Information, Recipient shall request that Discloser provide prior express written authorization of such use.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 explicitly requires the Recipient to ensure that its contractors, agents, suppliers, or advisors execute a non-disclosure agreement equivalent to the main agreement before disclosing Confidential Information. The changed text omits this requirement. Section 2 defines what constitutes Confidential Information and implicitly expects that it is protected from unauthorized disclosure. The omission in Section 5 directly contradicts this expectation because it no longer ensures that the Recipient's external parties are bound by confidentiality obligations before receiving such information. This creates a clear contradiction within the document, as the definition of confidential information in Section 2 implies a certain level of protection, which is undermined by the omission of the safeguarding clause in Section 5.
